Request Full Catalogue

Sustainable dining, made durable.

GreenGold designs and manufactures eco-friendly tableware pressed entirely from sugarcane bagasse — the fibrous stalk left over once the cane's juice is extracted. Where plastic and polystyrene persist for centuries, our plates, bowls and containers return to the earth within months, without compromising on strength for hot or cold food.

QualitySustainabilityInnovationIntegrity

Manufacturing excellence

Our production facility runs on advanced, automated machinery with strict in-house quality control — built to deliver safe, hygienic, and reliable tableware at scale, for both domestic and export markets.

  • ✓Automated production lines with in-house quality testing lab
  • ✓Hygienic packaging, handling, and bulk production capacity
  • ✓Custom product development for private-label orders
  • ✓On-time delivery across domestic and export markets

Frequently asked questions

What is bagasse, and how does it become tableware?

Bagasse is the fibrous pulp left over after sugarcane is pressed for its juice. We refine those leftover fibres into sheets, then mould them under heat into plates, bowls and containers — a renewable material that would otherwise go to waste.

Is it safe for hot food, the microwave, and the freezer?

Yes. Every product in the collection is food-grade, toxin-free, and rated safe for both microwave and freezer use, so it moves with the food from storage to table.

How long does it take to compost?

Under normal composting conditions, GreenGold tableware breaks down within a few months — far faster than plastic or polystyrene, and without leaving any residue behind.

Can we get custom sizes or private-label branding?

Yes — our facility supports custom product development, so sizing, packaging and private-label branding can be discussed for qualifying order volumes.

What is your production capacity?

Our facility runs at a capacity of 150 tons, supported by 150+ employees across 90,000 sq. m — with automated production lines and in-house quality testing on every batch.
